lines.english: Why did the policeman ask the writer to come to the police station?
lines.chinese: 警察为什么要叫这位作者到警察局去？
lines.raw: Why did the policeman ask the writer to come to the police station? | 警察为什么要叫这位作者到警察局去？

lines.english: It must have been about two in the morning when I returned home.
lines.chinese: 我回到家时，大约是凌晨两点钟。
lines.raw: It must have been about two in the morning when I returned home. | 我回到家时，大约是凌晨两点钟。

lines.english: I tried to wake up my wife by ringing the doorbell,
lines.chinese: 我按门铃想叫醒我的妻子，
lines.raw: I tried to wake up my wife by ringing the doorbell, | 我按门铃想叫醒我的妻子，
but she was fast asleep, | 但她睡得很熟，

lines.english: so I got a ladder from the shed in the garden, put it against the wall,
lines.chinese: 于是我从花园的小屋里搬来一架梯子，把它靠在墙上，
lines.raw: so I got a ladder from the shed in the garden, put it against the wall, | 于是我从花园的小屋里搬来一架梯子，把它靠在墙上，

lines.english: and began climbing towards the bedroom window.
lines.chinese: 开始朝着卧室的窗户爬去。
lines.raw: and began climbing towards the bedroom window. | 开始朝着卧室的窗户爬去。

lines.english: I was almost there when a sarcastic voice below said.
lines.chinese: 我快爬到窗户时，下面传来一个挖苦人的声音：
lines.raw: I was almost there when a sarcastic voice below said. | 我快爬到窗户时，下面传来一个挖苦人的声音：

lines.english: 'I don't think the windows need cleaning at this time of the night.'
lines.chinese: “我认为窗户在夜间这个时候不需要擦洗。”
lines.raw: 'I don't think the windows need cleaning at this time of the night.' | “我认为窗户在夜间这个时候不需要擦洗。”

lines.english: I looked down and nearly fell off the ladder when I saw a policeman.
lines.chinese: 我向下看去，当看到一个警察时，我差点从梯子上掉下来。
lines.raw: I looked down and nearly fell off the ladder when I saw a policeman. | 我向下看去，当看到一个警察时，我差点从梯子上掉下来。

lines.english: I immediately regretted answering in the way I did, but I said, 'I enjoy cleaning windows at night.'
lines.chinese: 我马上后悔不该那样回答，但我还是说：“我喜欢在夜间擦洗窗户。”
lines.raw: I immediately regretted answering in the way I did, but I said, 'I enjoy cleaning windows at night.' | 我马上后悔不该那样回答，但我还是说：“我喜欢在夜间擦洗窗户。”

lines.english: 'So do I,' answered the policeman in the same tone.
lines.chinese: “我也喜欢，”警察用同样的声调回答说。
lines.raw: 'So do I,' answered the policeman in the same tone. | “我也喜欢，”警察用同样的声调回答说。
Excuse my interrupting you. | “请原谅我打断你一下。

lines.english: I hate to interrupt a man when he's busy working, but would you mind coming with me to the station?'
lines.chinese: 我不愿意打断一个正在忙碌的人，但你介意跟我到警察局去一趟吗？”
lines.raw: I hate to interrupt a man when he's busy working, but would you mind coming with me to the station?' | 我不愿意打断一个正在忙碌的人，但你介意跟我到警察局去一趟吗？”

lines.english: 'Well, I'd prefer to stay here,' I said.
lines.raw: 'Well, I'd prefer to stay here,' I said. | “嗯，我宁愿呆在这儿，”我说。
'You see, I've forgotten my key.' | “您瞧，我忘了带钥匙。”
'Your what?' he called. | “你的什么？”他大声问。
'My key,' I shouted. | “我的钥匙！”我喊道。

lines.english: Fortunately, the shouting woke up my wife who opened the window just as the policeman had started to climb towards me.
lines.chinese: 幸运的是，我的喊叫声把妻子吵醒了，她打开窗户，而此时那个警察刚开始向我爬过来。
lines.raw: Fortunately, the shouting woke up my wife who opened the window just as the policeman had started to climb towards me. | 幸运的是，我的喊叫声把妻子吵醒了，她打开窗户，而此时那个警察刚开始向我爬过来。
